label: Architecture
description: Evaluates whether a skill library helps agents make sound architectural decisions — layering, dependency direction, ownership boundaries, composition, and coherence under scale.

tasks.prompt_text: Review the library and determine whether its skills help agents organize code into clear layers — with explicit contracts between layers, no leaking of implementation details, and predictable responsibility boundaries.
tasks.rubric_text:
0 no layering guidance
1 vague separation mention
2 some layer awareness but unclear contracts
3 clear layering with explicit inter-layer contracts
4 strong layering discipline with enforced abstraction barriers, contract definitions, and no-leak rules

tasks.prompt_text: Review the library and determine whether its skills help agents decide WHERE new code belongs — which layer, module, or file owns a piece of logic, and how to resolve ambiguous placement.
tasks.rubric_text:
0 no placement guidance
1 vague module mention
2 some placement awareness but no decision criteria
3 clear placement rules with ownership heuristics
4 strong placement discipline with decision trees, proximity rules, and explicit ownership resolution

tasks.prompt_text: Review the library and determine whether its skills enforce healthy dependency direction — higher-level modules depend on lower-level abstractions, not the reverse. No circular dependencies.
tasks.rubric_text:
0 no dependency direction awareness
1 vague import mention
2 some dependency awareness but allows circular deps
3 clear dependency direction rules with acyclic enforcement
4 strong dependency discipline with inversion of control, abstraction boundaries, and circular dep detection

tasks.prompt_text: Review the library and determine whether its skills help agents manage external dependencies — when to add them, when to inline, version pinning, and how to isolate third-party surface area.
tasks.rubric_text:
0 no external dependency guidance
1 vague package mention
2 some dependency management awareness
3 clear external dependency criteria with isolation patterns
4 strong dependency management with add/remove criteria, isolation wrappers, and version discipline

tasks.prompt_text: Review the library and determine whether its skills define clear ownership — every piece of logic has one unambiguous owner, with no shared-mutable-state or god-module anti-patterns.
tasks.rubric_text:
0 no ownership model
1 vague responsibility mention
2 some ownership awareness but ambiguous boundaries
3 clear single-owner discipline with anti-pattern detection
4 strong ownership model with explicit owner registry, no god modules, and ownership transfer protocols

tasks.prompt_text: Review the library and determine whether its skills help agents handle cross-cutting concerns — logging, auth, error handling — without scattering logic across every module or creating tight coupling.
tasks.rubric_text:
0 no cross-cutting guidance
1 vague middleware mention
2 some awareness but allows scatter
3 clear cross-cutting patterns with centralized handling
4 strong cross-cutting discipline with aspect isolation, minimal coupling, and explicit extension points

tasks.prompt_text: Review the library and determine whether its skills promote composition over inheritance — building complex behavior by combining small, focused units rather than deep hierarchies.
tasks.rubric_text:
0 no composition guidance
1 vague modularity mention
2 some composition awareness
3 clear composition-over-inheritance patterns
4 strong composition discipline with interface contracts, pluggable units, and explicit anti-inheritance guidance

tasks.prompt_text: Review the library and determine whether its skills help agents reason about architectural decisions under scale — what breaks when the codebase grows 10x, when the team grows, or when requirements shift.
tasks.rubric_text:
0 no scale reasoning
1 vague scalability mention
2 some growth awareness but no concrete analysis
3 clear scale reasoning with growth scenarios
4 strong scale discipline with explicit break-point analysis, team-size implications, and evolution paths
